Center: OECD/NEA Nuclear Data Bank, Boulogne-Billancourt, France
ref: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.547, p.165141 (2024)
title: Cross-section data for the reactions 12C(3He,p0)14N to 12C(3He,p6)14N at energies up to 6 MeV

x4freetext: Cross-section data for the reactions 12C(3He,p0)14N
to 12C(3He,p6)14N at energies up to 6 MeV

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: Nucl. Instrum. Methods in Physics Res., Sect.B, Vol.547, p.165141 (2024)

x4freetext: The 3-MV tandem accelerator.
The targets are composed of a 110-120 nm thick layer
of amorphous hydrocarbon (a-C:H) deposited on bulk
substrates made of chromium and silicon, with a
thickness of ~400 um and dimensions of 10 mm x 15 mm.
A thin layer of 3-5 nm gold was applied on top as a
reference for determining the cross-sections.
The silicon surface barrier detector with a
depletion depth of 2000 um, a solid angle of
30.3 +/- 1.2 msr, and an energy resolution of 22 keV at
135 degree.
A 5 um Ni foil and a 12 um Mylar foil placed in front
of detector to stop backscattered helium ions.
At 135 degrees, the lithium-drifted silicon
detector with a depletion depth of 2000 um, a solid
angle of 77.5 +/- 3.0 msr, and an energy resolution
of 40 keV.
At 175 degrees, an annular lithium-drifted silicon
detector of 2000 um, a solid angle of 19.5 +/- 0.8 msr
and an energy resolution of 30 keV.
A 50 um Mylar foil placed in front of detector to block
backscattered helium ions.
The main source for overall uncertainty are:
Statistical uncertainty of the proton
peak integrals (2.5-6%)
Statistical uncertainty of the 3He RBS
peak integrals (0.8-4%)
The ratio of areal densities of gold
to carbon (0.6-1.7%)
The detector solid angle ratio (0.5%)
Approved by L.Hess (2024-03-06)
